title: Greetings and divisions at Corinth
summary_text: Paul writes to God’s church at Corinth with all who call on the Lord’s name—grace and peace. He thanks God for their enrichment in speech and knowledge; they lack no gift as they wait for Christ’s revelation. He urges agreement without divisions; Christ was not divided—some were baptized in Paul’s name? Thanks—Christ sent him to preach the gospel not with wisdom of words lest cross be emptied.
key_points: You were enriched in everything in him.
That you all speak the same thing.
Is Christ divided?
Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News.
We preach Christ crucified.
evidence_phrases: To the assembly of God which is at Corinth
you were enriched in him
that you all agree
Is Christ divided?
Christ didn’t send me to baptize
we preach Christ crucified

title: Wisdom hidden; Spirit reveals; foundation
summary_text: Paul came not with excellence of speech—Jesus crucified demonstration of Spirit and power. God’s wisdom in mystery before ages—rulers crucified Lord of glory. We speak God’s wisdom in Spirit; natural man cannot know spiritual things. Milk not meat; God’s building; foundation Jesus Christ; temple of God is holy.
key_points: I came not with excellence of speech.
We speak wisdom among the perfect.
The natural man doesn’t receive the things of God’s Spirit.
You are God’s temple.
No other foundation can anyone lay than that which is laid—Jesus Christ.
evidence_phrases: I came not to you with excellence of speech
in demonstration of the Spirit and of power
We speak God’s wisdom in a mystery
The natural man doesn’t receive the things of the Spirit of God
You are God’s temple
For no one can lay any other foundation

title: Ministers and God who gives growth
summary_text: You are still flesh—envy, strife. Paul planted, Apollos watered—God gave growth. Ministers are servants, stewards of mysteries. All things are yours; you are Christ’s; Christ is God’s.
key_points: I couldn’t speak to you as spiritual, but as fleshly.
I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.
Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants?
All things are yours, and you are Christ’s.
evidence_phrases: I couldn’t speak to you as spiritual
I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase
We are God’s fellow workers
stewards of God’s mysteries
All things are yours

title: Stewards; apostles’ example
summary_text: Think of us as Christ’s stewards, faithful. Kingdom of God not in word but power. Apollos and Paul—ministers through whom you believed. We are fools for Christ; you are wise; we weak, you strong. We both hunger and thirst; we labor working with our hands.
key_points: It is required in stewards that a man be found faithful.
The Kingdom of God is not in word, but in power.
We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ.
We labor, working with our own hands.
evidence_phrases: servants of Christ, and stewards of God’s mysteries
required in stewards, that a man be found faithful
the Kingdom of God is not in word, but in power
we are fools for Christ’s sake
we both hunger and thirst, and are poorly clothed

title: Immorality and church discipline
summary_text: Sexual immorality reported—man with father’s wife; church puffed up. Remove wicked person; a little leaven leavens whole lump. Christ our Passover sacrificed; purge old leaven. Not to keep company with anyone called brother who is immoral.
key_points: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you.
Remove the wicked man from among yourselves.
Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?
Christ our Passover has been sacrificed for us.
For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside?
evidence_phrases: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you
Remove the wicked man from among yourselves
Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?
For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed for us
those who are outside, God judges

title: Lawsuits and the body as God’s temple
summary_text: Dare you go to law before unbelievers? Saints will judge world and angels. Wrongdoers will not inherit Kingdom. Body not for sexual immorality but for the Lord; body is temple of Holy Spirit—glorify God in body.
key_points: Dare any of you go to law before the unrighteous?
Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world?
The unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom.
Your bodies are members of Christ.
Your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit.
evidence_phrases: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor
Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world?
the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom
your bodies are members of Christ
your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit

title: Marriage, singleness, and devotion to the Lord
summary_text: Each have own wife/husband due affection; do not deprive except by consent for prayer. Unmarried care for Lord’s things; married care for spouse. Paul’s concession—not command—singleness good for present distress. Wife not bound if unbeliever departs.
key_points: Let the husband render to his wife the affection owed her.
Don’t deprive one another, unless by consent for a season.
He who is unmarried cares about the things of the Lord.
I wish all men were like me.
The unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife.
evidence_phrases: Let each man have his own wife
The husband should give to his wife her conjugal rights
Don’t deprive one another
He who is unmarried cares about the things of the Lord
The unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife

title: Idol meat and love
summary_text: Knowledge puffs up; love builds up. Idol is nothing; but some weak conscience defiled. If food causes brother to stumble, Paul will never eat meat. Bear weaknesses of weak, not pleasing ourselves.
key_points: Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.
We know that we all have knowledge.
An idol is nothing in the world.
Through your knowledge he who is weak perishes.
If food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forevermore.
evidence_phrases: Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up
we know that we all have knowledge
there is no God but one
through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes
if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forevermore

title: Paul’s rights and becoming all things
summary_text: Am I not free? Barnabas and Paul have right to eat and drink, take wife, reap material things? Yet Paul used none—lest he hinder gospel. Woe if he does not preach! Run to obtain incorruptible crown; buffet body.
key_points: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle?
If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?
I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them.
Woe is to me if I don’t preach the Good News!
I discipline my body and bring it into submission.
evidence_phrases: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle?
If we sowed to you spiritual things
I robbed other assemblies
Woe is to me if I don’t preach the Good News!
I run, therefore, in such a way
I discipline my body

title: Israel’s example; Lord’s cup; flee idolatry
summary_text: Israel baptized into Moses; drank spiritual Rock that was Christ—yet many fell. Let him who thinks he stands beware. Flee idolatry; cup of blessing communion of Christ’s blood; bread of body. All things lawful—not all profitable.
key_points: Now these things were our examples.
That Rock was Christ.
Flee from idolatry!
The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ?
Let no one seek his own good, but the good of his neighbor.
evidence_phrases: Now these things were our examples
that Rock was Christ
Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful
Flee from idolatry!
The cup of blessing which we bless
All things are lawful for me, but all things don’t edify

title: Head covering and Lord’s Supper
summary_text: Head of woman is man; head of Christ is God; woman prays prophesies covered. Lord’s Supper—divisions show who approved; eat discerning body; judgment for unworthy partaking; many weak and sick.
key_points: The head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man.
For this cause the woman ought to have authority on her head.
The Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.
For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself.
If we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.
evidence_phrases: the head of every man is Christ
the woman ought to have authority on her head
the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed
This is my body, which is for you
he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way
if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged

title: Gifts and one body
summary_text: No one speaking by Spirit of God calls Jesus accursed; no one can say Jesus is Lord except by Holy Spirit. Varieties of gifts, ministries, workings—same Spirit, Lord, God. Body many members, one body; greater honor to weaker parts; suffer together, rejoice together.
key_points: No one can say, ‘Jesus is Lord,’ but by the Holy Spirit.
There are various kinds of workings, but the same God.
For the body is not one member, but many.
God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part.
If one member suffers, all the members suffer with it.
evidence_phrases: no one can say, ‘Jesus is Lord,’ but by the Holy Spirit
there are various kinds of workings, but the same God
For the body is not one member, but many
God composed the body together
If one member suffers, all the members suffer with it

title: The way of love
summary_text: If speak tongues of men and angels without love—noise. Love suffers long, kind, not jealous, not proud, bears, believes, hopes, endures. Prophecies cease; tongues cease; knowledge passes—love never fails. Now faith, hope, love—greatest love.
key_points: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don’t have love, I have become sounding brass.
Love is patient and is kind.
Love never fails.
Now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face.
But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.
evidence_phrases: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels
Love is patient and is kind
Love never fails
For we know in part
then face to face
The greatest of these is love

title: Prophecy, tongues, and decent order
summary_text: Pursue love; desire spiritual gifts, especially prophecy. Tongues speak mysteries; prophecy edifies church. Unbeliever convicted by prophecy. Women silent in assemblies; all things decently and in order.
key_points: Follow after love, and desire spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy.
He who prophesies speaks to men for their edification.
All things must be done decently and in order.
God is not a God of confusion, but of peace.
Let all things be done decently and in order.
evidence_phrases: Follow after love
greater is he who prophesies
he who speaks in a language speaks not to men
God is not a God of confusion
Let all things be done decently and in order

title: Resurrection of Christ and the dead
summary_text: Gospel Paul preached—Christ died, buried, raised, appeared to many. If no resurrection, faith vain; we false witnesses. Christ firstfruits; then those Christ’s at coming; then end. How are dead raised? Spiritual body; must put on immortality—death swallowed in victory.
key_points: For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins.
If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain.
Christ has been raised from the dead, the first fruits of those who are asleep.
The last enemy that will be abolished is death.
Death, where is your sting?
evidence_phrases: Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures
he was buried
he has been raised on the third day
if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain
Christ has been raised from the dead, the first fruits
The last enemy that will be abolished is death
Death, where is your sting?

title: Collection, plans, and final exhortations
summary_text: Collection for saints—first day weekly giving. Paul will visit after Macedonia; Timothy and Apollos noted. Watch, stand in faith, be courageous, strong. Love; greet with holy kiss; curse on anyone who does not love the Lord.
key_points: Now concerning the collection for the saints
Let him that is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches.
Watch! Stand firm in the faith!
If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed.
Maranatha!
evidence_phrases: concerning the collection for the saints
On the first day of the week
Watch! Stand firm in the faith!
Let all that you do be done in love.
If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ
Maranatha!
